signing_authority: Not completed — yellow placeholder 'Your Name Here' in management assertion

system_description.overview: CoPlane is a cloud-based platform (described as 'AI-powered' or similar — exact tagline not stated in system description section). Hosted entirely on AWS, it provides a SaaS application serving customers over the internet with no physical office presence.

products.CoPlane: Core SaaS platform hosted on AWS; exact product category not elaborated beyond the system description section

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — physical security delegated to AWS as subservice organization)
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical office; fully cloud-hosted
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ — 3 availability zones used per region

network_architecture.network_segmentation: VPC WEST and VPC EAST with segmented subnets; ACLs used to control traffic between segments
network_architecture.firewalls: Network firewalls implemented, configured, and managed to control incoming/outgoing traffic based on predetermined security rules; firewall configurations reviewed regularly
network_architecture.ids_ips: Intrusion detection system monitors and detects unauthorized access or anomalies within the network; confirmed tested
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host used for VPN/remote access to production environment
network_architecture.tls: TLS used for data in transit; certificates from established CA
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic enters VPC EAST and VPC WEST; network segmentation via subnets and ACLs; bastion host for admin access; IDS monitors network; firewalls control perimeter

application_architecture.pattern: Not explicitly stated — likely microservices or modular given multi-region AWS deployment
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments maintained

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Not named — generic access control described
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all remote access to entity's information systems
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Policy and procedures established, documented, and enforced for password strength and confidentiality

encryption.at_rest: Encryption at rest implemented (KMS implied from AWS cloud controls)
encryption.key_management: Access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel; cloud KMS (AWS) implied

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git-based (specific platform not named — 'source code management tool' referenced)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Approval process followed prior to merging to master branch; developers cannot push directly to production without additional approval
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management / IaC used (specific tool not named)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management sets up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named — 'monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met'
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Log management established; logging enabled for administrative activities, log-on attempts, data deletions, function changes, security configurations, permissions, roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts to IT management for high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure (IaaS) — subservice organization under carve-out method

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-malware technology implemented, maintained, and regularly updated on production servers and workstations

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant changes to people/roles during period
CC3.2.1 — Cybersecurity insurance effectiveness could not be tested (no cyber incidents during period)
CC7.3.1 — Incident response could not be tested (no security incidents during period)
CC6.5.2 — Customer data deletion could not be tested (no customer terminations during period)
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical facility access controls excluded (AWS responsibility as subservice org)
A1.2 — Environmental protections excluded (no physical office; AWS responsibility)
notable_controls: RGEC (Risk and Governance Executive Committee) meets semiannually to review key internal controls
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly on security and operations
Network diagram formally maintained and reviewed annually by IT management (CC2.3.7)
Asset inventory maintained and updated on as-needed basis
Source code changes are logged, time-stamped, and attributed to author (CC5.2.5)
DR plan identifies critical IT programs, OS, personnel, data files, and time frames via business impact analysis

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-region (VPC EAST + VPC WEST), multi-AZ (3 AZs), IaC referenced. No CDN mentioned. Strong for an early-stage company.

application_architecture.rationale: No languages, frameworks, or compute types named. Separate environments and branch protection confirmed. Architecture pattern undisclosed — limited visibility.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups and multi-AZ confirmed. Data classification and retention policies exist. No database names, caching, or data warehouse mentioned.

security_posture.rationale: IDS, antivirus, firewalls, monthly vuln scans, annual pen test, MFA, cybersecurity insurance all confirmed. No WAF named. No SIEM named. Solid controls breadth.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, emergency change process, separate environments, IaC, and patch management all confirmed. No CI/CD tool named. No container orchestration mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, tested annually, multi-AZ and multi-region failover. Daily backups confirmed. No RTO/RPO disclosed.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS named as a specific vendor. No other third-party tools, SaaS platforms, or subprocessors named. High concentration risk on single cloud provider.

overall.rationale: SOC 2 Type 1 (point-in-time design only, not operating effectiveness). Solid infrastructure foundation with multi-region AWS, proper controls framework. Key gap: near-zero technology specificity — no tool names, no language/framework disclosure, no named SaaS vendors. The report uses generic ACCORP template language throughout with minimal company-specific content.

red_flags: Management assertion signing authority field left blank ('Your Name Here' yellow placeholder) — report appears to not be fully finalized/signed
Network diagram on page 18 is a 'Sample network diagram' placeholder watermark — company has not replaced it with their actual architecture diagram
Zero specific technology names disclosed beyond AWS — no database, CI/CD tool, monitoring platform, or identity provider named
SOC 2 Type 1 only — no operating effectiveness evidence; controls have never been independently tested over time
yellow_flags: No RTO or RPO stated for disaster recovery — unclear recovery commitments to customers
No WAF mentioned despite being a multi-region web application
Single cloud provider (AWS) with no secondary cloud or DR site outside AWS
No SSO mentioned — authentication provider not named
Vendor list extremely sparse — hard to assess third-party risk concentration
green_flags: Multi-region architecture (VPC EAST + VPC WEST) — strong for early-stage company
3-AZ deployment with automatic failover (spinning up multiple servers across all AZs)
MFA enforced for all remote access
Branch protection with approval required before merging to master
key_observations: This is an ACCORP PARTNERS templated report — the controls language is near-identical boilerplate across all ACCORP clients; very little company-specific technical content was injected
The placeholder network diagram and unsigned management assertion suggest the report was produced quickly and may not reflect mature security practices
The multi-region architecture is notably sophisticated for the apparent company size — either the team has strong infra experience or this is aspirational
No mention of any AI-specific security controls despite being an AI platform company (model security, prompt injection, data poisoning)
The report covers Security and Availability criteria but excludes Processing Integrity and Confidentiality — notable gaps for an AI product handling customer data
